This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Prior releases also added significant features:\\n- **1.2.0-beta.1**: Gates support, FleetMember labels support, TargetKubernetesVersion support, AOT-compatibility\\n- **1.1.0**: GA AutoUpgrade and AutoUpgradeProfile support (API version 2025-03-01)\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **Examples** section links only to the generic `.NET Management Library Code Samples` URL — no fleet-specific code examples exist in the README\\n- The **Key concepts** section links only to the generic Azure SDK intro with no description of Fleet-specific resource types (Fleet, FleetMember, UpdateRun, UpdateStrategy, AutoUpgradeProfile, Gates)\\n- No `tests/Samples/` directory exists — there are no test-backed snippets wired into the README\\n- None of the features added in recent releases (AutoUpgrade, Gates, FleetMember labels, TargetKubernetesVersion) are documented or demonstrated in the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File 1:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\nReplace the generic `## Key concepts` section with fleet-specific content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet` library provides access to the following resource types:\\n\\n- **ContainerServiceFleet** — the hub fleet cluster that manages member clusters\\n- **ContainerServiceFleetMember** — an AKS cluster enrolled in the fleet; supports labels and TargetKubernetesVersion\\n- **ContainerServiceUpdateRun** — an orchestrated Kubernetes version upgrade across member clusters, organized into stages and groups\\n- **ContainerServiceUpdateStrategy** — a reusable strategy template for update runs\\n- **ContainerServiceFleetUpdateStage** / **ContainerServiceFleetUpdateGroup** — define the rollout order and grouping of member clusters\\n- **AutoUpgradeProfile** — configures automatic upgrade channel (Rapid, Regular, Stable, NodeImage) for fleet members\\n- **Gates** — approval gates between update stages (requires human or automated approval before proceeding)\\n\\nFor an architectural overview, see the [Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nReplace the generic `## Examples` section with fleet-specific snippet placeholders:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following examples show common operations with the Kubernetes Fleet Manager library.\\n\\n### Create a Fleet\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Add a member cluster\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create an update run\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create an AutoUpgrade profile\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n**File 2:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/tests/Samples/Sample1_FleetBasicOperations.cs` — **create**\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_FleetBasicOperations : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateFleet()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateFleet\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = client.GetSubscriptionResource(\\n                SubscriptionResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId));\\n\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = (await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;)).Value;\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetCollection fleets = resourceGroup.GetContainerServiceFleets();\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetData fleetData = new ContainerServiceFleetData(AzureLocation.EastUS);\\n            ArmOperation operation = await fleets.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n                fleetData);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created fleet: {fleet.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task AddFleetMember()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:AddFleetMember\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            ResourceIdentifier fleetId = ContainerServiceFleetResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(\\n                \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = client.GetContainerServiceFleetResource(fleetId);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetMemberCollection members = fleet.GetContainerServiceFleetMembers();\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetMemberData memberData = new ContainerServiceFleetMemberData\\n            {\\n                ClusterResourceId = new ResourceIdentifier(\\n                    \\u0026#34;/subscriptions//resourceGroups//providers/Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters/\\u0026#34;),\\n                Group = \\u0026#34;staging\\u0026#34;\\n            };\\n\\n            ArmOperation operation = await members.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n                memberData);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetMemberResource member = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Added member: {member.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateUpdateRun()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateUpdateRun\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            ResourceIdentifier fleetId = ContainerServiceFleetResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(\\n                \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = client.GetContainerServiceFleetResource(fleetId);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceUpdateRunData runData = new ContainerServiceUpdateRunData\\n            {\\n                UpdateStrategySnapshot = new ContainerServiceFleetUpdateStrategySnapshot\\n                {\\n                    Stages =\\n                    {\\n                        new ContainerServiceFleetUpdateStage(\\u0026#34;stage1\\u0026#34;)\\n                        {\\n                            Groups = { new ContainerServiceFleetUpdateGroup(\\u0026#34;staging\\u0026#34;) }\\n                        }\\n                    }\\n                },\\n                ManagedClusterUpdate = new ContainerServiceFleetManagedClusterUpdate(\\n                    new ContainerServiceFleetManagedClusterUpgradeSpec(\\n                        ContainerServiceFleetManagedClusterUpgradeType.Full)\\n                    {\\n                        KubernetesVersion = \\u0026#34;1.30\\u0026#34;\\n                    })\\n            };\\n\\n            ArmOperation operation = await fleet\\n                .GetContainerServiceUpdateRuns()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, runData);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceUpdateRunResource updateRun = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created update run: {updateRun.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateAutoUpgradeProfile()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateAutoUpgradeProfile\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            ResourceIdentifier fleetId = ContainerServiceFleetResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(\\n                \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = client.GetContainerServiceFleetResource(fleetId);\\n\\n            AutoUpgradeProfileData profileData = new AutoUpgradeProfileData\\n            {\\n                Channel = AutoUpgradeChannel.Regular\\n            };\\n\\n            ArmOperation operation = await fleet\\n                .GetAutoUpgradeProfiles()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, profileData);\\n\\n            AutoUpgradeProfileResource profile = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created AutoUpgrade profile: {profile.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code Prior releases also added significant features:\\n- **1.2.0-beta.1**: Gates support, FleetMember labels support, TargetKubernetesVersion support, AOT-compatibility\\n- **1.1.0**: GA AutoUpgrade and AutoUpgradeProfile support (API version 2025-03-01)\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **Examples** section links only to the generic `.NET Management Library Code Samples` URL — no fleet-specific code examples exist in the README\\n- The **Key concepts** section links only to the generic Azure SDK intro with no description of Fleet-specific resource types (Fleet, FleetMember, UpdateRun, UpdateStrategy, AutoUpgradeProfile, Gates)\\n- No `tests/Samples/` directory exists — there are no test-backed snippets wired into the README\\n- None of the features added in recent releases (AutoUpgrade, Gates, FleetMember labels, TargetKubernetesVersion) are documented or demonstrated in the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File 1:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\nReplace the generic `## Key concepts` section with fleet-specific content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet` library provides access to the following resource types:\\n\\n- **ContainerServiceFleet** — the hub fleet cluster that manages member clusters\\n- **ContainerServiceFleetMember** — an AKS cluster enrolled in the fleet; supports labels and TargetKubernetesVersion\\n- **ContainerServiceUpdateRun** — an orchestrated Kubernetes version upgrade across member clusters, organized into stages and groups\\n- **ContainerServiceUpdateStrategy** — a reusable strategy template for update runs\\n- **ContainerServiceFleetUpdateStage** / **ContainerServiceFleetUpdateGroup** — define the rollout order and grouping of member clusters\\n- **AutoUpgradeProfile** — configures automatic upgrade channel (Rapid, Regular, Stable, NodeImage) for fleet members\\n- **Gates** — approval gates between update stages (requires human or automated approval before proceeding)\\n\\nFor an architectural overview, see the [Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nReplace the generic `## Examples` section with fleet-specific snippet placeholders:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following examples show common operations with the Kubernetes Fleet Manager library.\\n\\n### Create a Fleet\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Add a member cluster\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create an update run\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create an AutoUpgrade profile\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n**File 2:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/tests/Samples/Sample1_FleetBasicOperations.cs` — **create**\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. client.GetSubscriptionResource(\\n                SubscriptionResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId));\\n\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = (await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;)).Value;\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetCollection fleets = resourceGroup.GetContainerServiceFleets();\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetData fleetData = new ContainerServiceFleetData(AzureLocation.EastUS);\\n            ArmOperation operation = await fleets.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n                fleetData);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created fleet: {fleet.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task AddFleetMember()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:AddFleetMember\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            ResourceIdentifier fleetId = ContainerServiceFleetResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(\\n                \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = client.GetContainerServiceFleetResource(fleetId);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetMemberCollection members = fleet.GetContainerServiceFleetMembers();\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetMemberData memberData = new ContainerServiceFleetMemberData\\n            {\\n                ClusterResourceId = new ResourceIdentifier(\\n                    \\u0026#34;/subscriptions//resourceGroups//providers/Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters/\\u0026#34;),\\n                Group = \\u0026#34;staging\\u0026#34;\\n            };\\n\\n            ArmOperation operation = await members.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n                memberData);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetMemberResource member = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Added member: {member.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateUpdateRun()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateUpdateRun\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            ResourceIdentifier fleetId = ContainerServiceFleetResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(\\n                \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = client.GetContainerServiceFleetResource(fleetId);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceUpdateRunData runData = new ContainerServiceUpdateRunData\\n            {\\n                UpdateStrategySnapshot = new ContainerServiceFleetUpdateStrategySnapshot\\n                {\\n                    Stages =\\n                    {\\n                        new ContainerServiceFleetUpdateStage(\\u0026#34;stage1\\u0026#34;)\\n                        {\\n                            Groups = { new ContainerServiceFleetUpdateGroup(\\u0026#34;staging\\u0026#34;) }\\n                        }\\n                    }\\n                },\\n                ManagedClusterUpdate = new ContainerServiceFleetManagedClusterUpdate(\\n                    new ContainerServiceFleetManagedClusterUpgradeSpec(\\n                        ContainerServiceFleetManagedClusterUpgradeType.Full)\\n                    {\\n                        KubernetesVersion = \\u0026#34;1.30\\u0026#34;\\n                    })\\n            };\\n\\n            ArmOperation operation = await fleet\\n                .GetContainerServiceUpdateRuns()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, runData);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceUpdateRunResource updateRun = operation.Value;\\n verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateAutoUpgradeProfile()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateAutoUpgradeProfile\\n            ArmClient client = profileData = new AutoUpgradeProfileData\\n            {\\n                Channel = AutoUpgradeChannel.Regular\\n            };\\n\\n            ArmOperation operation = await fleet\\n                .GetAutoUpgradeProfiles()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, profileData);\\n\\n            AutoUpgradeProfileResource profile = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created AutoUpgrade profile: {profile.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code It also added scenario test coverage (`FleetCRUD.cs`) exercising CRUD operations for ManagedNamespace. The unreleased `1.2.0-beta.2` CHANGELOG section was not updated.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG not updated**: The `1.2.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)` section under `### Features Added` is empty despite ManagedNamespace support being added\\n- **README Examples section is a stub**: The `## Examples` section only links to generic SDK samples; no concrete code examples or snippets exist for any Fleet resource types\\n- **ManagedNamespace not mentioned in README**: The new `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource` type, its CRUD operations, and key concepts (DefaultResourceQuota, DefaultNetworkPolicy, etc.) are completely absent from the README\\n- **No Samples test file**: There is no `tests/Samples/` directory with snippet-backed samples; the `## Examples` section cannot link to testable code examples\\n\\n\\u003cdetails\\u003e\\n\\u003csummary\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\u003c/summary\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit the `1.2.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)` section to add the ManagedNamespace entry:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.2.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Added `ManagedNamespace` resource support (`ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource`, `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection`, `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData`), enabling creation, update, deletion, and enumeration of managed namespaces within a Fleet.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n1. **Create** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`\\n\\n   Provide at minimum two snippet-backed samples:\\n   - `Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateFleet` – authenticate, create a Fleet resource\\n   - `Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateManagedNamespace` – create a ManagedNamespace under an existing Fleet\\n\\n   Example structure:\\n\\n   ```csharp\\n   // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation.
